DISCOVERY 612, HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT GENENTECH, INC.'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO COMPEL RETURN OF PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS AND TO PRECLUDE THE DEPOSITION OF DENISE SMITH-HAMS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS TO COMPEL THE RETURN OF PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS. THE DOCUMENTS THAT ARE THE SUBJECT OF THE MOTION CONTAIN PRIVILEGED MATERIAL. ALL UNREDACTED DOCUMENTS AND ALL COPIES OF THOSE DOCUMENTS SHALL BE RETURNED OR DESTROYED WITHIN 5 DAYS OF THIS HEARING. ALL UNREDACTED DOCUMENTS THAT WERE FILED IN THIS MOTION SHALL BE WITHDRAWN FROM THE PUBLIC RECORD AND SEALED. NOONE SHALL HAVE ACCESS TO THOSE DOCUMENTS UNLESS THIS ORDER IS APPEALED AND THE COURT OF APPEALS ORDERS THEM PRODUCED FOR THEIR REVIEW. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS IT SEEKS AN ORDER PRECLUDING THE DEPOSITION OF DENISE SMITH-HAMS. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Compel Return Of Privileged Documents And To Preclude Deposition Of Denise Smith-Hams, Appendix
Off Calendar Jul-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Lynne C. Hermle, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Genentech, Inc.
Elizabeth Madden, Atty. for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Natalia Kirnos

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Genentech, Inc.'s Motion For Protective Order To Compel Return Of Privileged Documents And To Preclude The Deposition Of Denise Smith-Hams is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as it seeks to compel the return of privileged documents. The documents that are the subject of the motion contain privileged material. All unredacted documents and all copies of those documents shall be returned or destroyed within 5 days of this hearing. All unredacted documents that were filed in this motion shall be withdrawn from the public record and sealed. Noone shall have access to those documents unless this order is appealed and the Court of Appeals orders them produced for their review. Plaintiff's request for in camera review is denied.

The motion is denied as it seeks an order precluding the deposition of Denise Smith-Hams.

All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Veronica H. Garcia, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Natalia Kirnos
Michael A. Aparicio, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Genetech, Inc.

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Natalia Kiros' Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents From Defendant is granted. As to Interrogatory No. 27, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The response is incomplete. If the exact dates in February and March are unknown, the response must state that there has been a reasonable and good faith inquiry made to obtain the information. The objections to Request For Production Of Documents Number 52 are overruled and ordered stricken, except as to attorney client and work product privilege. Further responses and production shall be made no later than May 15, 2009. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Mtoion And Motion To Compel Further Responses, Privilege Log And Production Of Documents From Pltf Natalia Kirnos
Off Calendar Oct-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Michael Aparicio, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Genentech, Inc.
Veronica Garcia, Esq., for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Natalia Kiros

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Genentech, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses, Privilege Log, And Production Of Documents From Plaintiff is granted in part and moot in part. The motion is granted as it seeks an order overruling the following objections: overbroad, compound, and does not describe the category of documents with sufficient particularity to inform responding party of the documents sought; and the objection that there are no documents which are not otherwise equally available to Defendant.
The motion is granted as to Request Numbers 38, 39, 40, and 41. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further and to produce documents within 15 days of notice of this order.

The motion is moot as it seeks a privilege log. Counsel are advised that the arguments made by Plaintiff regarding a privilege log have no merit.
ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
